description_long: This picnic table is constructed from several wide, flat wooden planks arranged parallel to each other, forming a rectangular tabletop. The table is supported by four sturdy, rectangular wooden legs positioned at each corner, providing stability and a classic, rustic appearance. The wood has a rich, medium-brown finish with visible grain patterns, giving it a natural and inviting look suitable for outdoor environments. There are no attached benches, making it a standalone table that can accommodate separate seating. The design is simple and robust, with no decorative elements or additional features such as an umbrella hole. The overall structure suggests durability and functionality, ideal for communal outdoor use.
description: A sturdy, rectangular wooden picnic table with a plank-style top and four solid legs, finished in a medium-brown wood tone. The table has no attached benches or umbrella hole, emphasizing a simple and functional design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow end of the table with three visible wooden planks and two sturdy legs.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the tabletop with four supporting legs and the plank arrangement clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, showing the opposite narrow end with the same plank and leg configuration.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the full tabletop and leg structure visible.
attribute_tabletop_support_structure: The tabletop is supported by four rectangular wooden legs positioned at each corner, directly attached to the underside of the tabletop planks for stability.
